description_long: This sculpture depicts two human figures, one larger and one smaller, entwined in a dramatic, dynamic pose. The larger figure appears to be holding or supporting the smaller figure, with both bodies partially draped in flowing fabric that adds a sense of movement. The surface is textured and weathered, suggesting it is made from stone or concrete, and the details of the musculature and facial expressions are finely rendered. The top of the sculpture is irregular and broken, possibly indicating damage or an unfinished aspect. The base is broad and stable, supporting the vertical composition of the figures. The coloration is predominantly a mix of gray and beige tones, with some darker areas accentuating the depth and contours.
description: A detailed stone sculpture of two entwined human figures, one supporting the other, with flowing drapery and a weathered, textured surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing both figures clearly, with the larger figure supporting the smaller one and the flowing drapery visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the sculpture and the curvature of the figures, with less detail visible on the faces.
description_view_2: Opposite side view showing the muscular back of the larger figure and the positioning of the smaller figure at the base.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the backs of both figures and the drapery, with the base and the broken top more apparent.
attribute_subject_matter: two entwined human figures in a dramatic pose
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the background is plain and does not indicate an environment.
